title: container create failed under podman 4.6.1 and conmon 2.1.8
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22595
Originally posted by **x2byue** May 3, 2024 Does anyone hit this error with podman 4.6.1 and conmon 2.18? podman run --rm -it test Error: container create failed (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes "": readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||... podman version Client: Podman Engine Version: 4.6.1 API Version: 4.6.1 Go Version: go1.20.12 Built: Fri Apr 19 04:09:18 2024 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 conmon --version conmon version 2.1.8 commit: 57ed23ee47beaf9a72b28f5666fab095a9ad4a38

comments.body:
Its a generic error you can narrow it down a little bit more by using `--log-level=debug` and strace

I'm stuck in a similar situation (#22274), all forward upgrades will break it and there is no temporary file I know of that could hinder it. Mine started happening at 4.7.2 - yours could be a different issue if you look into it with above suggestions.

The message in the debugging log does not make sense to me. The file oom_score.adj is owned by the host user who launched Podman.

INFO[0000] Running conmon under slice user.slice and unitName libpod-conmon-8997af1f0922652e3b46045e5a749ffd4c98541f872d5f111835e9ec77de9933.scope
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

ls -al /proc/self/oom_score_adj
-rw-r--r-- 1 s-dmadminua documentum-ua 0 May  6 09:23 /proc/self/oom_score_adj

I set up a Podman-Desktop with Podman 5.02 on Windows. It can build the existing dockerfiles and run containers successfully. Unfortunately, the Podman 5.0.2 is not available for RHEL 8.x. It is scheduled to be released for RHEL 9.5

podman run --log-level debug --hostname dasvr --name dasvr --rm -d -p 8180:8080 --memory 8g dasvr

Its a generic error you can narrow it down a little bit more by using --log-level=debug and strace

I'm stuck in a similar situation (#22274 [github.com]<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/github.com/containers/podman/issues/22274__;!!MLsdJ25-fIk!qQtnSvgUdupEZpd1rSQ1XTrCBF00A4cJRuyPshr3iBgx3oAdPrFNRDTOiP_-SWP-zs33aEXV5NKM5EEPDXBrfnyeFp8$>), all forward upgrades will break it and there is no temporary file I know of that could hinder it. Mine started happening at 4.7.2 - yours could be a different issue if you look into it with above suggestions.

—
Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub [github.com]<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/github.com/containers/podman/issues/22597*issuecomment-2094180722__;Iw!!MLsdJ25-fIk!qQtnSvgUdupEZpd1rSQ1XTrCBF00A4cJRuyPshr3iBgx3oAdPrFNRDTOiP_-SWP-zs33aEXV5NKM5EEPDXBrEOnnUi0$>, or unsubscribe [github.com]<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/BAI5L4HWXATOOZBZ3FDSYJLZATNXFAVCNFSM6AAAAABHF6KTS2VHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV43OSLTON2WKQ3PNVWWK3TUHMZDAOJUGE4DANZSGI__;!!MLsdJ25-fIk!qQtnSvgUdupEZpd1rSQ1XTrCBF00A4cJRuyPshr3iBgx3oAdPrFNRDTOiP_-SWP-zs33aEXV5NKM5EEPDXBr9fn_nbA$>.
You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID: ***@***.******@***.***>>

First, please use the bug template and provide the information requested there.
Second, "runc create failed: unable to start container process: unable to setup user: invalid argument" is you error in the log you provided. To me the log sounds like a invalid subuid/subgid setup so not a really a bug
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#10-rootless-setup-user-invalid-argument

Lastly if you encounter issues in RHEL then you should go through the Red Hat support and not upstream on github.
